Anurag
Mishra

Public Education  |  Governance Reforms  |  Technology Policy

Policy practitioner with experience across government, multilateral development, and institutional reform. I work on the systems that shape how people access quality education, develop workforce skills, and participate in economic life — with a focus on India and emerging economies.

View Resume Read Writing Explore Projects
Areas of Work

What I Work On

My work spans four interconnected policy domains, each grounded in questions of delivery, governance, and equity.

Education Reform

Improving learning systems through policy design, foundational literacy and numeracy, governance reform, and evidence-based delivery.

Skills & Workforce

Designing skilling systems, evaluating national programmes, and facilitating labour mobility — with a focus on rural and informal workers.

Governance & Delivery

Strengthening institutional capacity, public systems, and the implementation architecture that connects policy design to on-the-ground outcomes.

AI & Society

Examining how AI and digital technology reshape public systems, workforce participation, and the governance of social infrastructure.

Current Thinking

What I'm Working On

Questions and themes I'm actively researching, writing about, and exploring through ongoing project work.

01

The Future of Vocational Education

How should national skilling systems respond to rapid occupational churn? What role do ITIs and community colleges play in the next decade of labour market transitions in India and the Global South?

02

AI in Public Systems

As governments adopt AI tools for service delivery, hiring, and policy analysis, what governance structures protect against bias and exclusion? How should public institutions build AI literacy internally?

03

Workforce Development in Emerging Economies

How do labour mobility frameworks, recognition of prior learning, and results-based financing interact to determine who can access productive employment — and who is left behind?

04

State Capacity and Implementation

What does it actually take for ambitious policy to be implemented well? The bureaucratic, political, and financial infrastructure of effective public administration in developing states.

Open to collaboration

Research, policy consulting, speaking, or just a conversation about shared interests.

Get in touch anurag.mishra@columbia.edu

Anurag Mishra

Policy professional — Education, Skills & Governance

I work at the intersection of public systems, institutional reform, and evidence — with a focus on education, workforce development, and governance in India and emerging economies. My career has moved between government, multilateral development institutions, and now graduate research, giving me an unusually direct view of the distance between policy design and implementation reality.

Before Columbia, I served as Chief of Staff to the Minister of Education for Delhi, where I managed operations across approximately 1,000 public schools and 1.8 million students. I co-led the institutional design and launch of Delhi Skills and Entrepreneurship University. Prior to that, I worked as a consultant at the World Bank's Global Education Practice, contributing to programme evaluations, governance frameworks, and analytical work across skilling, early childhood, and rural livelihoods.

At Columbia SIPA, I'm a Dean's Scholar and CPRL Merit Scholar. I consult with Connecticut school systems on teacher retention reform and support Columbia Global's strategy and AI adoption work. My research interests centre on skills systems, political economy of reform, and the governance of public service delivery.

Current Affiliation
Columbia University, SIPA MPA Candidate — Dean's Scholar

Expertise

Areas of Work

Skilling & Workforce Policy

National programme evaluation, Recognition of Prior Learning systems, results-based financing, and international labour mobility frameworks.

Education Governance

School system reform, foundational literacy and numeracy, institutional design, and monitoring and evaluation of large-scale public programmes.

Development Finance

Results-based lending (DLIs/KPIs), multilateral programme design, and costed reform scenario development in education and social sectors.

Government Operations

Ministerial office management, multi-stakeholder coordination, strategic communications, and reform programme execution in complex political environments.

Policy Research & Writing

Implementation completion reports, concept notes, market feasibility studies, and policy briefs for government, multilateral, and civil society audiences.

Data & Analytics

Survey design and analysis, programme M&E, dashboard development, and quantitative methods in R and Python applied to education and labour data.

Selected Work

Highlights

Featured Work

World Bank / Ministry of Rural Development, India 2025

Rural Prosperity & Resilience Programme: Skills Architecture

A three-part analytical series covering sector identification, international skill mapping across 14 economies, and a diagnostic of India's Recognition of Prior Learning system with portability recommendations.

Shaped the skills and livelihoods architecture for the World Bank's rural prosperity programme in India.

Skills Policy Policy & Advisory
World Bank Group 2023-26

Skill India Mission Operation: Implementation Completion Report

Co-authored the World Bank ICR for a $250M federal skilling initiative covering approximately 10 million beneficiaries. Translated field evidence and outcome data into operational and policy recommendations.

Role: Co-author of the published World Bank Implementation Completion Report.

The published ICR directly informed the design of successor national skilling investments in India.

Research & Evaluation Skills
World Bank / MSDE, India 2024-25

ITI Modernisation Programme Design

Contributed to the KPI/DLI framework, governance architecture, and costed reform scenarios for a $3.6B programme to modernise approximately 1,000 Industrial Training Institutes across India.

Helped structure one of the largest vocational education reform programmes in the world by value.

Program Design Governance
Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi 2021-23

Delhi Education Reform and DSEU Launch

As Chief of Staff to the Education Minister, oversaw programme design, execution, and performance monitoring across 1,000 public schools serving 1.8 million students. Co-led the institutional design and launch of Delhi Skills and Entrepreneurship University with 35 programmes and approximately 10,000 students.

One of the largest state-level education reform operations in India, combining school system management with the creation of a new public university.

Education Reform Program Design
Policy & Advisory

Strategy, advisory outputs, and analytical work tied directly to policy decisions.

World Bank / Ministry of Rural Development, India 2025

RPRP Skills Architecture: Sector Identification, International Mapping, and RPL Diagnostic

Three policy notes for the Rural Prosperity and Resilience Programme. Identified emerging employment sectors using FutureSkills Prime data, mapped skill demand across 14 priority destination economies, and assessed the portability of India's RPL system with reform recommendations.

Saving $200 Billion: Implementing the "Once Only" Policy in the USA

Memo to the US President proposing a pilot of Estonia's "once only" digital governance principle — applied to death notifications and birth registrations — to reduce improper federal payments.

Policy Themes

Topics

An intellectual map of my work — organised by policy domain rather than chronology.

Education Reform

Improving how public education systems are designed, governed, and delivered — from early childhood through secondary schooling. The focus is on foundational learning, institutional accountability, and the conditions that allow effective teaching at scale.

Key Questions

What enables foundational literacy and numeracy gains to persist at scale, not just in pilots?
How do political incentives shape education reform — and how can reform be designed to be resilient to them?
What does quality preschool look like in resource-constrained, high-density public systems?

Related Work

Project
Delhi Education Reform — Chief of Staff
Report
State of the Nation: FLN in India
Project
Connecticut Teacher Residency Design
Project
NECTAR Programme Supervision, Nagaland

Skills & Workforce Development

Building systems that enable people to access productive work — including national skilling programmes, recognition of prior learning, international labour mobility, and the occupational standards that anchor them.

Key Questions

How should skilling systems respond to rapid occupational churn without becoming training mills?
What makes Recognition of Prior Learning credible — to employers, workers, and receiving countries?
How can outward labour migration be structured so that it benefits rural workers, not just remittance aggregators?

Related Work

Report
SIMO Implementation Completion Report
Policy Note
RPRP Emerging Sectors Framework
Concept Note
RPL System Diagnostic — India
Project
ITI Modernisation Programme Design

Governance & Delivery

The implementation architecture that connects well-designed policy to on-the-ground outcomes. This includes state capacity, results-based financing, bureaucratic incentive structures, and the role of data in public accountability.

Key Questions

What institutional conditions allow ambitious reform to survive political transitions?
How do DLI and KPI frameworks in results-based lending actually change behaviour — or fail to?
When does digitisation of public services improve delivery, and when does it just move exclusion upstream?

Related Work

Project
Delhi Education Reform Operations
Memo
"Once Only" Digital Governance — USA
Project
ITI KPI / DLI Framework Design
Project
Columbia Global Strategy Work

AI & Society

How AI and digital infrastructure reshape public systems, labour markets, and governance — and what institutional design is needed to make these transitions equitable rather than exclusionary.

Key Questions

How should public institutions build genuine AI literacy — not just tool adoption?
What governance structures are needed when AI enters hiring, benefit eligibility, and service delivery?
How does AI-driven automation interact with skilling policy in labour-surplus economies?

Related Work

Project
AI Adoption Programme — Columbia Global
Ongoing
AI in Public Systems — Research Interest
